qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST project-user deletion authorization bypass lets a manager remove higher-privileged members
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the REST project-user deletion path in ProjectUsersDeleteCommand, while the advisory targets SOAP login in mci_check_login() and a MySQL-specific password type-conversion authentication bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable SOAP authentication code or its MySQL-specific password type-checking flaw.
evidence: The advisory patch modifies api/soap/mc_api.php:mci_check_login() to enforce ?string types on the SOAP username and password parameters and coerce null values before authentication, explicitly to prevent the MySQL-specific SOAP authentication bypass.

The only qualifying session finding is a REST authorization bug in api/rest/restcore/projects_rest.php and core/commands/ProjectUsersDeleteCommand.php where the delete-user flow checks the actor's own project access level instead of the target user's level before calling project_remove_user().

These findings differ in endpoint, file, vulnerable function, prerequisite, and root cause: SOAP login type confusion on password handling versus authenticated REST authorization validation failure on project membership deletion.
